completeness_checks.note: All 2,148 NICE TKS elements (946 tasks, 662 knowledge, 540 skills) evaluated. No exclusions — unlike STRM-001-ONET which excluded 43 physical abilities, NICE contains no element types requiring categorical exclusion.

rationale_documentation.note: Every rationale file validated as well-formed JSON with complete method coverage. All 1,720 scored files contain STS primary + 4 secondary methods (BERTScore, NLI, Bi-Encoder, Jaccard). All 428 no-relationship files have methodology field documenting the exclusion rationale.

relationship_type_consistency.subset_of_note: No 'Subset of' relationships assigned. Expected: NICE elements are broader cybersecurity competencies; WIDAI KSAs are domain-specific. The absence of Subset relationships is consistent with WIDAI being the more specialized framework in this mapping.
relationship_type_consistency.strength_mapping_consistency_check: PASS — Equal relationships have highest mean strength (6.64), followed by Superset (5.78), then Intersects (4.73). This ordering is logically consistent: Equal pairs should score highest, Superset pairs should score higher than Intersects because the WIDAI KSA is fully contained within the NICE element.

strength_scoring.scoring_methodology: Cross-Encoder STS (stsb-roberta-base) as primary, raw 0-1 mapped to 0-10 integer

mapping_strength_rationale_consistency.note: All strength values in strm_mapping.json match corresponding rationale files.

comparison_to_strm_001.interpretation: STRM-002 scores 0.38 points lower on average than STRM-001. Expected: O*NET elements are general occupational descriptors with broader semantic overlap to any professional framework, while NICE elements are cybersecurity-specific with domain vocabulary divergence from WIDAI's data/AI terminology. Lower mean strength is consistent with cross-domain mapping between non-overlapping professional domains.

potential_misclassifications_detail.description: Knowledge of data de-identification tools and techniques
potential_misclassifications_detail.assessment: Borderline — registered as NICE-GAP-001. De-identification has legitimate WIDAI relevance but no matching WIDAI KSA exists, so 'No relationship' is technically correct. The gap register captures the implication.
Correctly excluded — NICE IR is cybersecurity-specific. However, the absence of data/AI IR competencies in WIDAI is registered as NICE-GAP-002.

potential_misclassifications_detail.description: Knowledge of incident response roles and responsibilities
potential_misclassifications_detail.assessment: Correctly excluded — cybersecurity IR roles. Gap signal registered in NICE-GAP-002.

potential_misclassifications_detail.description: Knowledge of organizational cybersecurity incident response plans
potential_misclassifications_detail.assessment: Correctly excluded — cybersecurity-specific. Gap signal registered in NICE-GAP-002.

no_relationship_validation.note: All 428 no-relationship classifications are defensible. Four borderline items were reviewed and assessed as technically correct — the gap implications are captured in the gap issue register rather than reclassification.

cross_framework_corroboration.NICE-GAP-004_corroborates_ONET-GAP-004: Process Maturity Assessment (NICE) corroborates Project and Time Management (O*NET). Both frameworks surface operational maturity gaps in WIDAI from independent directions.
cross_framework_corroboration.note: First cross-STRM evidence accumulation. This corroboration strengthens the case for both gaps during Phase 1D Synthesis.

overall_assessment.summary: STRM-002-NICE completed with full coverage of 2,148 NICE TKS elements. Multi-method scoring pipeline (identical to STRM-001-ONET: STS primary + 4 secondary) processed 1,720 scored pairs and generated 2,148 rationale files with content-specific significance — each referencing actual shared vocabulary, unique terms, and concept-level analysis. All validation checks pass. Relationship type distribution is logically consistent. Strength scores are appropriately lower than STRM-001 due to cross-domain vocabulary divergence. Five gap signals identified — two moderate, three low — with one cross-framework corroboration (NICE-GAP-004 ↔ ONET-GAP-004).
